text: Last summer, I planned a trip to Japan for two weeks. Before leaving, I spent hours researching flights, hotels, and must-see places. Once there, I discovered a whole new culture—temples, local customs, and polite greetings were everywhere. The food was incredible: fresh sushi, steaming ramen, and sweet mochi. However, language barriers were tricky; I often pointed at menus or used a translation app. Despite the challenges, those moments made the adventure memorable. Now, I cherish the memories—long walks through Tokyo streets, trying new dishes, and laughing with locals. That trip taught me that travel is about embracing the unknown.
